presented: Intake scope: Cintas (CTAS) acquiring UniFirst (UNF); status unconfirmed (424B3 seed doc suggests a real proposed/signed deal); all headline terms are null seeds; depth standard; no emphasized areas. Flagged status must be confirmed in research and all terms are seeds not facts.
steering: Scope looks right - proceed with default coverage (all required + recommended areas), no extra emphasis.

presented: Source plan: 32 sources across 11 areas + 9 deal-specific key_questions. Discovery reframed the deal (2025 rejected $275 cash bid -> Engine Capital activist proxy fight -> 2026 $310 cash+stock; FTC Second Request issued Jun 11 2026). Pinned DEFM14A, Engine PREC14A/DEFC14A, FTC 8-K, financing bridge, 2025 precedent PR, investor deck, Vestis 10-K. Flagged timeline discrepancy (spec says 2023; public bid was Nov2024-Mar2025), several search-hint (unpinned) sources, court_dockets skipped, trade_press down-weighted.

Research brief (262 verified / 14 quarantined; 10/12 areas, 0 gaps). After approval, ran 4 gap-fills per analyst: (1) self-manufacture vs outsourced supply-chain model; (2) how the $375M cost synergies get realized + whether the business supports it (cost structure + G&K base rate); (3) why Cintas is so persistent (~4yr, >=5 approaches); (4) the ~4 wearers-per-customer anomaly. Read the Dec 2025 investor deck locally (image-PDF; management framing only). Brief re-rendered with 4 new sections.
steering: Brief looks great, comprehensive. Fill 4 gaps (supply-chain model + what it means for Cintas; cost-synergy realization mechanism; Cintas's persistence; wearers:customers ~1:4 anomaly). Also read the local investor deck. Moving to expert: reason carefully so conclusions entail their premises.

presented: Preliminary memo (8 sections, 15 expert inference claims). Analyst gave two rounds of detailed feedback: plain prose (no cryptic taglines), facts-first then one inference, self-sufficient doc, explain cost-of-revenue/SG&A, take a view on the plants, valuation table with per-share ranges + offer-vs-range, descriptive ownership split, scope the verdict; plus 5 clarity fixes (divestiture wording, merchandise amortization, density-vs-customer-overlap, the divestitures point, 'close vs value case').
steering: Rewrote memo to address all feedback; also applied pipeline/skill fixes (PR #12) so future runs reflect it. Approved moving to red-team.

presented: Final memo post red-team: 12 skeptic findings (2 high, 5 medium, 5 low); 10 accepted with edits, 2 held; 5 rt_ inference claims added; 2 key-questions closed from the fact base. 284 verified / 14 quarantined.
